LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF CITI-NETWORKING GROUP, INC'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION - PLAINTIFF SEEKS RECONSIDERATION OF AN ORDER EXECUTED BY JUDGE ROBERTSON ON JULY 22, 2014, WHEN JUDGE ROBERTSON WAS SITTING IN THE LAW AND MOTION DEPARTMENT. THEREFORE, THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 15, 2014 AT 1:45 PM IN DEPT. 502 TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE ROBERTSON. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONTINUED TO JULY 22, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. THE JULY 1, 2014 ORDER GRANTING THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER IS AMBIGUOUS AS TO WHETHER THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE PERTAINS SOLELY TO PRESERVING EVIDENCE, OR ALSO APPLIES TO THE OTHER RELIEF SOUGHT FOR ACCESS AND PASSWORDS. AT THE JULY 22, 2014 HEARING, THE COURT WILL CONSIDER WHETHER A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD BE ISSUED FOR ALL THE RELIEF SOUGHT IN PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER. DEFENDANT MAY FILE A SUR-REPLY, NOT EXCEEDING 15 PAGES, BY JULY 17, 2014 THAT RESPONDS TO PLAINTIFF'S REPLY BRIEF AND EVIDENCE. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. (302)
DECLARATION OF DERRICK A RAMIREZ IN SUPPORT OF SURREPLY RE OSC WHY PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED FILED BY DEFENDANT RAMIREZ, DERRICK A
POS OF SUR-REPLY RE WHY PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT ISSUE; RE. FOR JUDICIAL NTC; AND DEC FILED BY DEFENDANT RAMIREZ, DERRICK A
ORDER (GRANTING IN PART AND DENYING IN PART) RE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOLD NOT ISSUE

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER REQUIRING DEFENDANT DERRICK A. RAMIREZ TO PRESERVE EVIDENCE SHALL REMAIN IN EFFECT DURING THE PENDENCY OF THESE PROCEEDINGS. ALL OTHER REQUESTED RELIEF IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT IT HAS AN OWNERSHIP INTEREST IN THE EQUIPMENT PURCHASED AND CONFIGURED BY DEFENDANT AND THEREFORE, HAS NOT ESTABLISHED A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976. (302)

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Ex Parte Papers Are Deemed Moving Papers; Opposition Due 7/07; Reply Due 7/09)
Off Calendar Jul-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert S. Fletcher, Esq. of Fletcher Law Practice, Tel. 415.254.0863, for plaintiff;
Janelle J. Smith, Esq. of Stall Parker LLP, Tel. 510.545.4980, for defendant.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is granted in part and denied in part. The temporary restraining order requiring Defendant Derrick A. Ramirez to preserve evidence shall remain in effect during the pendency of these proceedings. All other requested relief is denied. Plaintiff has not established that it has an ownership interest in the equipment purchased and configured by Defendant and therefore, has not established a likelihood of success on the merits. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Ex Parte Papers Are Deemed Moving Papers; Opposition Due 7/07; Reply Due 7/09)
Off Calendar Jul-11-2014 Continued To Jul-22-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction is continued to July 22, 2014. The July 1, 2014 Order granting the Temporary Restraining Order is ambiguous as to whether the Order to Show Cause pertains solely to preserving evidence, or also applies to the other relief sought for access and passwords. At the July 22, 2014 hearing, the Court will consider whether a preliminary injunction should be issued for all the relief sought in Plaintiff's Ex Parte Application for a Temporary Restraining Order. Defendant may file a sur-reply, not exceeding 15 pages, by July 17, 2014 that responds to Plaintiff's reply brief and evidence.
Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/PJB
